prerequisites: A Datadog account with access to Organization Settings, on the correct regional site (for example datadoghq.com for US1 or datadoghq.eu for EU1).
An organization-level API key (the api_key field). Organizations must maintain between 1 and 50 API keys.
A user-scoped Application key (the application_key field), created by a user with permission to create application keys.
The site parameter matching the Datadog region your organization is on (for example datadoghq.com, us3.datadoghq.com, us5.datadoghq.com, datadoghq.eu, ap1.datadoghq.com, or ddog-gov.com).

authMethods.name: API key and Application key
authMethods.summary: Datadog API requests authenticate with two credentials used together: an organization-level API key (api_key) and a user-associated Application key (application_key). The API key identifies the organization, and the Application key grants programmatic access, inheriting the creating user's permissions unless restricted with authorization scopes. Both are required by this connector.

setupSteps.title: Create an API key
setupSteps.body: In Datadog, go to Organization Settings and select the API Keys tab. Click New Key, enter a unique name, and click Create API key. Copy the value and use it for the connector's api_key field.

setupSteps.title: Create an Application key
setupSteps.body: Go to Organization Settings > Application Keys. Click New Key, enter a non-blank name, and click Create Application Key. Secure the key immediately, because organizations with One-Time Read mode enabled display the secret only once. Allow a few seconds for the key to propagate, then use it for the application_key field. Scope the key to the minimum access needed if your organization uses authorization scopes.

setupSteps.title: Set the Datadog site
setupSteps.body: Set the site config field to the parameter for your organization's region, since each Datadog site is independent. Examples: datadoghq.com (US1), us3.datadoghq.com (US3), us5.datadoghq.com (US5), datadoghq.eu (EU1), ap1.datadoghq.com (AP1), or ddog-gov.com (US1-FED).

setupSteps.title: Configure sync options
setupSteps.body: Optionally set start_date and end_date to bound the time range, provide query or queries to select the data to pull, and set max_records_per_request to control batch size. These fields are optional.
